items.title: Roche ends work on tominersen after decade of trials, as well as early-stage Huntington’s asset
items.description:
After more than a decade of work and plenty of ups and downs, Roche has dropped its Huntington’s disease drug tominersen.

 The decision comes after tominersen did no better than placebo in a Phase 2 ...

items.description:

items.title: GSK walks away from Alector pact as immuno-neurology bet fizzles
items.description:

items.title: STAT+: ARPA-H launches $160 million effort to develop custom gene editing drugs
items.description: An ARPA-H program to jump-start custom gene editing treatments was delayed by a change in administrations. Now seven labs will get sizable grants.
